results.possibleIdentity: Michael Grotzky is an engineer and technical professional who was active in the New York area during the late 1960s and 1970s. He is documented as a participant in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) Technical Service Program, a matching service that connected artists with engineers and scientists. His E.A.T. profile (indexed on a technical service card) lists his employer as Speciality Electronics and his location as Monsey, New York. His technical expertise spans a wide range of fields relevant to media arts and industrial engineering, including electrical engineering, light equipment/projection, strobes, electroluminescence, neon, ultraviolet, fiber optics, acoustics, sound generation, audio systems, audio-visual conversions, communications, television cathode rays, video tape, recording, photography, remote control, circuit design, transistors, and environmental simulation. Professionally, he is also associated with the development of technical reports and software tools in the Rockland County area; archival records show a John Grotzky (potentially a relative) active in software engineering in Monsey during the 1990s. Biographically, Michael Grotzky married Paulette Karkowski (1944–2009) in a civil ceremony at New York City Hall in 1971. The couple lived in the New York area before moving to Putnam Valley, New York, in 1979, where they resided for several decades. They have a daughter, Anna Grotzky, born in 1978.

The couple lived in the New York area before moving to Putnam Valley, New York, in 1979, where they resided for several decades.
